title: eupatorium tetrastichum
intro: Eupatorium tetrastichum, also known as the American feverfew or bitterweed, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antipyretic effects, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Eupatorium tetrastichum include reducing fever, alleviating headaches and body aches, and treating various digestive issues, such as diarrhea and nausea. Herbal preparations of Eupatorium tetrastichum can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to make medicinal remedies for colds, flu, and other common ailments.
